author: nashatyrev
content:
From my understanding the peer scoring is mostly up to implementation. If a client is too restrictive then it would likely struggle finding and maintaining a pool of peers so this is kind of self-regulating. 
Not well aware of the Prysm implementation in particular, but the logic could be to downscore  offline nodes to reduce the number of further reconnect attempts. If it prevents inbound connections from this node, then it's likely a bug.

author: tersec

author: tersec
content: a bit of a leading question. I'll just say that, EIP-8061 is part of Glamsterdam, EIP-8071 not, and looks like EIP-8080 also not. Obviously the relative priority or recognition or agreement different people might have of whether there's a "loophole in consolidations" to "fix" is subjective and individual

content: In general these are discussed in biweekly ACDC calls, but to be fair, while those are public, they tend to be core dev-oriented
